text: Fill in the blanks on pelvic wall and viscera anatomy
questions: The pelvic diaphragm is formed primarily by the *levator ani* muscle and the coccygeus muscle.
The *puborectalis* sling maintains the anorectal angle of approximately 90 degrees, which is essential for faecal continence.
The nerve supply to the levator ani comes from the nerve to levator ani, derived from *S3* and S4 nerve roots.
The *internal iliac* artery is the main arterial supply to the pelvic viscera, pelvic walls, and perineum.
The female pelvis is classified as *gynaecoid* type with a rounded inlet and a subpubic angle greater than 90 degrees.
In benign prostatic hypertrophy (BPH), the *median/middle* lobe of the prostate enlarges and obstructs the internal urethral orifice.
The *sacral* plexus is formed by the ventral rami of L4-S4 and gives rise to the sciatic nerve.
Uterine prolapse results from weakening of the *levator ani* muscle and the cardinal and uterosacral ligaments.
The pelvic inlet is bounded posteriorly by the *promontory* of the sacrum.
Suprapubic cystostomy is performed *two/2* cm above the pubic symphysis when the bladder is distended, avoiding the peritoneum.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
Excellent! You have a strong grasp of this topic.
